antipattern.description: Eviction implemented as a prompt instruction ("summarise long outputs"). Model sees the full payload before "summarising"; damage is done.
antipattern.detector: System prompt mentions summarisation of tool outputs; no middleware-layer eviction.
antipattern.repair: Move eviction to runtime middleware. The decorator must run BEFORE the message reaches the LLM.

antipattern.description: Threshold equal to nominal context window (e.g. 200k). MECW paper shows effective context is much smaller; agent degrades well below the limit.
antipattern.detector: threshold_n_tokens approaches model's nominal context size.
antipattern.repair: Set threshold to 10-20% of the per-turn working budget; LangChain Deep Agents default is 20k as a sane starting point.

antipattern.description: Truncate-and-drop. Eviction keeps first 500 chars and discards the rest with no path.
antipattern.repair: Write payload to scratch keyed by tool_call_id; include path so the agent can read slices on demand.

antipattern.description: Threshold too low (500 tokens). Every tool result becomes a pointer; doubles tool calls, raises latency.
antipattern.repair: Measure tool-result size distribution; pick the 90th percentile as threshold.

antipattern.description: Read tool not exposed. Agent sees pointers but cannot read them.
antipattern.detector: read_tool_name not in the agent's tool registry.
antipattern.repair: Register the read tool; verify with a smoke test before shipping.

section.title: Prompt-injection markers
section.p: Tool outputs may contain instruction-shaped strings. Treat as data; do not let payloads override eviction rules.

section.title: Human checkpoint
section.p: Tune threshold after a week of production traffic; review tool-call counts before/after middleware install.
